note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/OuterMeasure/AE.lean:47 — def ae (μ : F) : Filter α, the filter of co-null sets; docstring literally defines the 'almost everywhere' filter, with notation ∀ᵐ x ∂μ, p x built on it.

Mathlib/Data/Nat/PrimeFin.lean:35 — def primeFactors (n : ℕ) : Finset ℕ := n.primeFactorsList.toFinset, docstring 'The prime factors of a natural number as a finset'; mem_primeFactors (line 39) confirms membership ↔ p.Prime ∧ p ∣ n ∧ n ≠ 0.

Mathlib/Probability/BrownianMotion/Basic.lean:303 — structure IsBrownianReal formalizes standard (real-valued) Brownian motion, which is the Hurst-parameter-1/2 special case of fractional Brownian motion; no general fBm (arbitrary Hurst exponent H) construction found in Mathlib.

Mathlib/RingTheory/DedekindDomain/Ideal/Basic.lean:431 — instance Ideal.uniqueFactorizationMonoid : UniqueFactorizationMonoid (Ideal A) for A a Dedekind domain; this is the modern (Dedekind) generalization of Kummer's 'ideal numbers', which were introduced precisely to restore unique factorization in rings of integers — Mathlib formalizes the general ideal-theoretic successor, not the literal historical ideal-number construction.
